original_problem: In a small village, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the switches on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb?
deconstructed_problem: In a small village, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the switches on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb?
original_steps: Step 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding bulb.
Step 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position, then immediately enter the room.
Step 3: Observe the bulbs to identify which one is on (corresponds to switch 2), which one is off but warm (corresponds to switch 1), and which one is off and cold (corresponds to switch 3).
Step 4: Based on the observations, determine the correct switch-bulb mapping.
Step 5: Verify the solution by checking the consistency of the switch-bulb mapping with the initial conditions.
skeleton_steps: ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding bulb.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position, ତାହେଲେ (then) immediately enter the room.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3: Observe the bulbs to identify which one is on (corresponds to switch 2), which one is off but warm (corresponds to switch 1), and which one is off and cold (corresponds to switch 3).
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 4: Based on the observations, determine the correct switch-bulb mapping.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 5: Verify the solution by checking the consistency of the switch-bulb mapping with the initial conditions.
final_answer: Switch 1 corresponds to the warm but off bulb, switch 2 corresponds to the on bulb, and switch 3 corresponds to the cold and off bulb.
